quote_of_day.text: Protest watch thread, so that you dont have to make a new thread over something. Use this as a mega thread for posting live updates or just general discussion. Im having a nice coffee and watching it all burn down
quote_of_day.name: Anon

articles.title: Weather on the board today
articles.body:
A low-pressure system of anti-BJP sentiment moved across the board, fueled by calls for Dharmendra Pradhan's resignation.

Anon argued that his resignation would be ideal for the BJP, as the fear instilled in people was now destroyed.

A scattered shower of protest discussion developed, with one anon creating a mega thread for live updates and general discussion. Meanwhile, a cold front of criticism formed around Dharmendra Pradhan, with some anons demanding his resignation.

A light drizzle of speculation about 2029 plans and discussions about Stacies at protests added to the overcast conditions, with one anon asking anti-Modi groups about their plans and another discussing the presence of Stacies at protests.

articles.title: Dharmendra Pradhan's Resignation Demanded
articles.body:
Anon claimed that Dharmendra Pradhan's resignation would be the most ideal outcome for the BJP, as the fear instilled in people is now destroyed.

Anon stated that even if 5% of the population decides to go against the BJP, it would be a nightmare for them.

One anon argued that the BJP is not the same as other parties, citing infrastructure development and improvements in the education system.

Another anon countered that all parties are the same, and the problem lies in politicians being driven by power and personal gain rather than the nation's or people's interests.

Anon asserted that the BJP has made India strong, powerful, and competitive, while other parties want to keep India weak and dependent on others.

Vector claimed that the government will not take accountability until the situation becomes out of hand, and that once they take some accountability, people will ask for more.

Anon stated that there is little corruption in the BJP, and the results can be seen in the infrastructure built.

Another anon claimed that the NEET scandal has nothing to do with the BJP and that the party has improved India's education system. Anon also mentioned that other countries, like China, have successfully curbed corruption.

Lala Larper Rai predicted that the BJP will not yield to the demand for Pradhan's resignation, as he is the second most popular leader in Odisha after Naveen Patnaik, and the party wants him to appease the bhim dehatis.

Anon joked about someone masturbating, prompting another anon to advise musaljeetas to evacuate immediately. One anon expressed concern that the party lacks internal correction mechanisms to self-regulate from corruption and treachery.

Another anon asked how to get paid to shill for the BJP, while an anon claimed that Modi is very popular, and his popularity is driven by envy or low IQ from liberal abbos.

Anon stated that the BJP could have won easily if they had realized that people in India can be insane. Another anon accused someone of being an IT cell worker, covering up the fact with word-salad faggery.

Anon also claimed that nobody needs to pay them to support Modi, and they would do it for free, showcasing loyalty that abbos and leftists cannot understand.

articles.title: Protest Watch Thread Sparks Discussion
articles.body:
Anon started a protest watch thread, inviting others to post live updates and discuss the ongoing protests. Anon claimed to be watching the protests while having a coffee, and enjoying the show.

One anon stated that most middle-class or poor people are not against corruption, they are just mad that they are not part of the "in" group.

This anon also mentioned that the same protesters would engage in corruption if given the chance, and that paper leaks have been happening for decades. Another anon mentioned that people are smarter and better organized, but extreme violence happens when groups splinter.

This anon also wondered if molotovs and other violent acts have started happening. Anon claimed that every revolution in the world involved a transfer of wealth from one rich group to another, and that it was never about the masses.

Another anon responded, saying that as long as rich people are punished, it's good enough, even if nothing changes.

Some anons discussed the possibility of government toppling, with one stating that it usually happens when a subset of rich people gets angry at the rich people in charge.

Another anon said that the quality of life for the masses did improve after the transfer of power in some cases.

Several anons expressed concerns about the safety of protesters, with one saying that they wouldn't attend due to the risk of getting hurt or charged at with a laathi.

Another anon mentioned that the government might be hiding information about dead protesters, and that the only reports might come from social media posts or parents of missing or injured people.

Anon also speculated about the potential consequences of a terror attack or an attack by Pakistan on J&K, saying it would lead to utter chaos. Another anon believed that the protest was intended to destabilize the country, rather than bring about positive change.

articles.title: Anon Calls for Dharmendra Pradhan's Resignation
articles.body:
Anon called for Dharmendra Pradhan's resignation, stating he must resign without providing a reason.

Another anon responded, saying nobody cares about accountability and that the government has already taken action by catching those responsible for the leak, cancelling the exam, and reconducting it.

Anon claimed a girl was a Muslim because a guy was asking her about Eid when she was an atheist, and being an atheist publicly in Pakistan can result in death.

Another anon found the girl's social media profiles, including her LinkedIn and Instagram accounts, and shared the links. The girl's boyfriend's Instagram account was also found.

Some anons discussed the girl's background, with one stating she seemed like a rich Muslim woman from Mumbai or Pune, and another saying she was ugly compared to white women.

One anon joked that Pakistan was becoming modern, referencing the girl's relationship with a Hindu boyfriend. Another anon said rich people are the same everywhere and societal norms do not apply to them.

The conversation took a turn when anons started discussing their own personal lives, with one saying he was a decade older and a NEET, and another stating that love is not meant for them but at least fun is.

Anons also made comments about the girl's relationship, with one saying Hindu men were drilling Muslim women. The discussion was filled with derogatory language and personal attacks.

articles.title: Anon Asks Anti-Modi Groups About 2029 Plans
articles.body: Anon asked anti-Modi groups how they plan to cope when Modi wins again in 2029. One respondent claimed that pre-2014, over 50% of India practiced open-defecation, until someone gave them toilets and access to water.

articles.title: Anon Discusses Stacies at Protests
articles.body: Anon asked where the stacies were at the protest, saying they were busy decomposing gobar. One anon claimed professions with little opposite gender contact, like gynecology, are the least randi professions.
